groups.context_description: Inner condition of unresolved division — will suspended between two incompatible orientations, committed to neither
Anxious inner preoccupation with material provision, bodily needs, and future security — orientation away from trust toward self-concerned worry
Inner state of anticipatory anxiety in the face of threat — worry about what to say or do under pressure
Divided or directed inner orientation of care — for the things of God or for worldly matters; also genuine care for others
Inner state of anticipatory pre-worry — anxiety about a future threatening situation before it arrives
Inner orientation of active expectation and watchful waiting — person directed toward what is coming, with hope, longing, or anxiety
Eschatological expectation as governing inner orientation — whole inner life directed toward and shaped by what God has promised
Anxious unsettling thoughts pressing upon the heart — inner person as site of accumulated care and disturbance
Anxious preoccupation with worldly concerns that competes with and displaces inner spiritual receptivity
Inner burden of care for others carried as expression of relational and pastoral orientation
Expectation as an inner state with affective weight — anticipation carrying dread, foreboding, or intensity of directed attention
Inner condition of ease and security — freedom from dread as positive state, often fruit of trust or divine promise
Complacent ease — inner condition of undisturbed settledness that has become spiritual stagnation
Inner condition of freedom from anxious care — the state of being untroubled as a desirable orientation of the inner person
Inner condition of relief from anxiety — reduction of grief-laden concern through wellbeing of a beloved person
Term names God's truth as a quality of his character and word — the ground on which the inner being anchors its trust and hope
Term names truth-speaking as a moral disposition of the inner being — speaking truth as a character quality that reflects and shapes the person
Term names truth as the content of a verified report or declaration — factual certainty as the basis for inner discernment and decision
Term names the inner act of whole-person assent — the affirmation of the inner being that something is true, right, and binding; consent given with the whole self
Term names certainty or reliability as the ground on which the inner being rests — the sure foundation that enables trust and security
Term names the inner orientation toward genuine truth — the person pressing inward toward what is real, whether in wonder, theological questioning, or moral challenge
Term names the inner act of trust in God — the person's inner orientation of faith expressed in faithfulness of character and reliance on God
Term names faithfulness as the quality of God's inner purposive reliability — the sure foundation of his plans and deeds
Term names the negation of worship and allegiance — the inner refusal to serve foreign gods; exclusive inner devotion to God expressed through the sustained refusal to bow, serve, or comply
Term names the negation that attests inner moral integrity — the person in whom no fault, error, or dishonour can be found; blamelessness as the character of the inner life
Term names the negation of inner humility and honour toward God — the heart that has not humbled itself; pride as an inner orientation asserted through the negation of what was owed to God

Term names the negation of self before God — inner humility expressed by denying personal merit, wisdom, or standing; creaturely nothingness before divine sovereignty; spiritual capacity and trust vindicated through divine action
Term names the negation of inner rest and composure — inner disturbance and distress disclosed through the absence of sleep, diversion, or peace
Term names the covenantal negation of belonging — the condition of estrangement in which identity as God's people is withdrawn; the inner-being experience of being declared not-mine
Term names the negation of belief or trust — the refusal or failure of the inner act of faith toward God
Term names the inner state of anguish, despair, and self-loathing — Job's negations of inner peace, rest, confidence, and desire to live
Term names God's negated inner states — his inner dispositions withheld, withdrawn, or denied: love, delight, mercy, anger restrained
Term names spiritual blindness or unresponsiveness — the inner failure to know, perceive, return to, or cry from the heart to God
Term names inner moral integrity affirmed through negation — the person who does not sin, does not fear, acts from the fear of God
Term names the inner grief or distress disclosed through negation — the person who conceals or expresses inner sadness, relational rupture, or covenantal estrangement
Term names the inner act of believing — the person's inner orientation of trust directed toward God, his word, or his messenger
Term names faithfulness as inner character — the quality of reliability, steadfastness, and trustworthiness as a disposition of the person or of God
Term names trust withheld or broken — the inner state of refusal to believe, inability to trust, or the collapse of confidence
Term names the inner collapse of the person under reproach — the condition of despair in which the heart is broken, pity is absent, and the person is without comfort
Term names the condition of devastation as the context for an inner failure of response — the desolation that no one receives into the heart; the gap between outer ruin and inner recognition
Term names despair as the complete collapse of inner expectation and hope — the inner being brought beyond what it can sustain, unable to see forward
Ruthlessness as inner moral character — the disposition of those who do not set God before themselves
The ruthless as oppressor — the inner experience of being under ruthless domination and longing for deliverance
Dread of God — the inner terror and trembling before divine majesty
Dread as anticipated inner terror — the fear that comes upon the heart before disaster
Dread falling on enemies — the inner terror of nations before God or his people
The absence or presence of dread as inner moral condition — the wicked without fear of God; the righteous without terror
Boasting in human might — the inner moral failure of self-reliance and pride
The Lord as mighty warrior — inner confidence and boldness grounded in divine presence
The heart of warriors overcome — inner terror and collapse under God's judgement
Term names reverence for God as an inner disposition that expresses itself in good works and proper conduct
Term names the inner condition of being disheartened — the faint, broken, or discouraged state of the inner person under pressure or affliction
Term names the inner condition of fearful timidity associated with deficient faith — cowardly fear that displaces trust
Term names cowardice as a moral inner condition — the fearful person as a moral category alongside the faithless and detestable
Term names the reverential trembling that others display before a person of authority — an inner-being response of awe and deference
Term names the quality that produces inner awe — the overwhelming, awesome quality of beauty or power that generates wonder-fear
Term names the quality that produces inner dread — the terrifying character of an enemy or overwhelming power
Term names terror as a divinely-imposed inner condition — the overwhelming dread placed upon creatures before a superior power
Term names inner dismay — the condition of being broken and shattered in spirit by overwhelming fear or defeat
Term names terrors — the overwhelming inner fears that beset a person, particularly in the face of mortality and the unknown
Term names the inner alarm that produces withdrawal — the fearful inner response to witnessing another's suffering that causes the observer to pull back
Term names the inner confusion and bewilderment of mind that comes as divine judgment — the dissolution of rational orientation in the person
Term names the startled astonishment that overtakes a person before the unexpected and miraculous — an inner-being response of shock at divine intervention
Term names the inner condition of dread-filled trembling before God — the fearful, cowering inner posture of those who encounter divine power
Term describes the shuddering horror that seizes a person before catastrophic destruction — an inner-being terror expressed in involuntary somatic response
Term names the horror that seizes bystanders witnessing catastrophic divine judgment — an inner-being state of appalled dread before overwhelming destruction
Term names the trembling that overtakes the inner being under the assault of dread or terror — the inner-somatic response to overwhelming fear
Term describes the bodily-inner trembling that overtakes a person in the presence of divine holiness or the weight of sin before God
Term names overwhelming inner terror before divine presence or theophanic encounter
Term names inner alarm triggered by moral and judicial reality — the fear that arises when confronted with divine judgment or its implications
Term names the inner condition of anxiety — the worried, anxious state of the inner person in the face of uncertain or threatening circumstances
Term names inner sorrow over sin — the grief and contrition of the person who acknowledges wrongdoing
Term names the inner orientation of reverent devotion — the disposition that characterises the God-fearer and true worshiper, producing faithful attendance to the divine
Term names the inner act of worship directed at God or idols — reverent devotion that can be genuine or misdirected, fruitful or vain
Term names the inner condition of fearful disturbance — the troubled, afraid state of heart that Christ's peace displaces
Term names the devout person characterised by inner godly reverence — the inner moral quality of reverential fear expressed in faithful, careful living
Term names the disposition of reverential fear toward God that is owed but absent — the inner orientation Israel has forfeited through apostasy
Term names the overwhelming inner awe that arises in response to divine action, presence, or miracle — the fear-and-wonder that leads to worship and praise
Term names the inner orientation of godly fear — the reverential awe before God that shapes conduct, holiness, and ministry
Term names the practical inner fear before threatening powers, death, or judgement — the protective or enslaving fear that may need to be overcome or redirected
Term names the inner condition of fear before what is frightening — the intimidated inner state that godly courage displaces
Term names the fear of the Lord as the foundational inner orientation of wisdom — the beginning and source of knowledge, wisdom, life, and understanding
Term names the fear of the Lord as the proper inner orientation of worship and covenant obedience — reverential awe before God that shapes conduct, praise, and faithful living
Term names terror as divine instrument or existential dread — the fear God places on peoples, or the acute inner anguish before overwhelming threat
Term names the terror that God imposes as an instrument of power — the inner dread placed by God upon peoples, nations, and creatures before his purposes
Term names the reverential fear of God as the proper inner orientation — the awe and dread that God is owed as master and father, expressed in covenant fidelity
Term names the terror that God imposes as an instrument of power — the inner dread placed by God upon peoples, nations, and creatures before his purposes
Term names the reverential fear of God as the proper inner orientation — the awe and dread that God is owed as master and father, expressed in covenant fidelity
Term names the inner fears that become the instrument of divine judgement — what the faithless dread is brought upon them as the consequence of their rejection
Term names the inner condition of fearful dread — the fears that oppress the person, whether delivered by God or brought upon oneself by wickedness
Term names the quality of divine judgment or presence as terror-inducing — the inner state of dread that the encounter with the holy God produces
Term names terror-producing events or phenomena — the things that generate overwhelming inner fear at the end of the age
Term names the inner condition of fear before a powerful enemy — those whose power generates inner dread in the one who faces them
Term names the inner condition of fearless freedom — serving, speaking, and acting without fear as the positive inner state of liberation through God
Term names the absence of godly fear as an inner moral failure — feasting without reverent restraint as the inner condition of the shameless
Term names the God-fearer as one whose inner orientation of reverence and obedience opens divine attentiveness
Term names the inner state of panic that seizes the person or community, immobilizing composure and overthrowing resolve
Term names reverence as the inner posture of godly fear before God — the disposition that makes prayer heard and worship acceptable
Term names the shuddering horror that seizes the inner being under overwhelming dread, appalling revelation, or imminent catastrophe
Term names the inner state of overwhelming terror before divine presence — the total inner overturn of the person in the face of the holy
Term names terror sent by God as an instrument of divine power — the overwhelming inner dread God places on enemies before Israel
Term names the terror of God experienced by the individual — the inner condition of acute dread before divine presence or judgment
Term names terror as the quality of overwhelming power — the dread that powerful persons, creatures, or situations generate in others
Term names the comprehensive inner terror of being surrounded on every side — the overwhelming existential condition of fear from which there is no escape
Term names the inner spirit of timidity — the fearful, cowardly disposition that God has not given, contrasted with power, love, and self-control
Term describes the inner astonishment, wonder, or stupefaction that overwhelms a person before the extraordinary acts of God or the collapse of expected order
Term names reverential awe before divine power — the inner posture of standing in awe of God, called for from all creatures
Term names inner dread before a threatening power — the practical fear of persons, nations, or forces that can harm
Term describes the inner experience of dread or terror under threat — a fear that destabilizes, paralyzes, or consumes the person inwardly
Term describes the inner orientation of reverential fear and awe toward God — the fear that is the proper and blessed response to divine majesty, word, and goodness
Term describes the absence or displacement of fear — inner peace, confidence, and security that replaces dread through trust in God
Term names the theophanic or miraculous inner fear — the overwhelming inner response to divine presence, miracle, or power, leading to awe and worship
Term names the reverential fear of God as the inner orientation of godly life — fearing God who judges both body and soul; the inner disposition that shapes worship and ethics
Term names the practical or self-interested inner fear before humans or consequences — the inner calculation of social, political, or physical danger that drives action
Term names commanded reverence before God — the inner orientation of trembling fear before the living God, imposed by royal decree
Term names the inner terror produced by visions, powers, or threatening presences — the overwhelming inner dread before what is terrifying or incomprehensible
Term names the inner condition of fearful dread — the apprehensive inner state before what one fears may befall oneself
Term names the inner disposition of fear that is commanded against in the context of divine reassurance and the uniqueness of God
Term names divine command not to fear — the divine assurance 'Fear not / Do not be afraid' addressed to individuals in crisis, granting inner peace through the declaration of divine presence
Term names the reverential fear of the Lord as the proper inner orientation — fearing God rather than humans as the inner disposition of covenant loyalty, worship, and moral conduct
Term names the practical inner fear of threatening powers — the inner condition of dread before enemies, authorities, or circumstances that threaten life or wellbeing
Term describes the inner dissolution of heart and spirit under overwhelming fear, dread, or threatening circumstance — the collapse of resolve and courage before a superior force or divine presence
Term names reverential fear that produces faithful obedience — the inner disposition of awe-driven trust that acts on divine warning
Term names practical inner fear for physical safety — the inner assessment of danger that produces protective action
Term names trembling before divine theophanic presence — the total inner agitation produced by the overwhelming encounter with God
Term names trembling in fear before threatening powers or circumstances — the inner agitation of panic and dread
Term names the inner condition of promised security — dwelling without trembling; the eschatological rest from fear as the divine gift
Term describes the inward disposition of dread or terror before a threatening force — a fear that collapses resolve and drives flight or paralysis; the commanded opposite of courage
Term describes the inner orientation of holy reverence and awe before God — the fear that is the proper response to divine majesty and holiness
Term names the inner state of trembling fear — an overwhelming inner agitation produced by the presence of the divine or the terrifying, expressing itself in the body
Term names the inner condition of awe-filled trembling before divine encounter — the overwhelming inner agitation that seizes those at the boundary of the sacred
Term names the inner disposition of trembling reverential fear in godly service — the humble, dependent inner attitude that characterises authentic Christian obedience and ministry
Term names the inner condition of overwhelming trembling before the sacred or the divine — the total inner agitation that seizes those at the boundary of the holy
Term names the inner condition of panic and dread — the acute inner terror before threatening circumstances, battle, or judgement
Term names the inner condition of trembling at the word of God — the responsive, reverent trembling that characterises the person God looks upon with favour
Term names the inner condition of fearful trembling before threatening circumstances or the sacred — the inner agitation of fear or reverent anxiety
Term names the inner-somatic trembling that characterises the human response to the presence and power of God — whether as fearful collapse in the guilty or as the posture of reverential worship
Term names shame as an inner state that either inhibits action or is overcome by inner confidence and hope — the person choosing not to be ashamed as an act of inner identity confidence
Term names shame as the inner experience of disgrace and disappointment — the person overwhelmed by shame from moral failure, social reversal, or spiritual abandonment
Term names the promised absence of shame for those who trust in God — hope in God is the antidote to inner shame; shame will not be the final word for the faithful
Term names the shaming of enemies and evildoers as divine judgment — God bringing inner shame upon those who oppose him or his people
Term names the inward experience of being ashamed — the felt condition of shame, confoundedness, or disgrace arising from failure, defeat, or exposure
Shame as personal inner experience — being ashamed or confounded as a felt state before God or others, including its refusal or absence as moral indicator
Shame as socially imposed disgrace or relational dishonour — being disgraced, reproached, or put to dishonour by others; protecting from or inflicting humiliation
Shame as theological reversal — confounding of opponents or prophetic-eschatological reassurance that God's people shall not be put to shame
Term names contempt as an inner attitude of disregard — held toward others from pride or comfortable indifference, or experienced as relational humiliation
Term names derision — the condition of being an object of mocking contempt among enemies, arising from moral-spiritual failure within the community
Term names utter, extreme disgrace — the condition of total inner-social degradation as the antithesis of glory, pronounced as divine judgment
Term names dishonor as an inner-social condition — the state of disgrace that accrues to persons through foolish, wicked, or morally compromised conduct
Term names dishonour as an inner-relational state — the moral condition of disgrace affecting the inner person's standing, endured or imposed
Term names indecency as the expression of inner moral disorder — shameless acts arising from disordered inner desires, and exposure of inner spiritual nakedness
Term names reproach as an inner-social condition experienced by individuals — the felt shame of being despised, scorned, or dishonoured before others
Term names reproach as a corporate-communal condition imposed upon God's people as divine judgment or national disgrace — the community bears the inner-social burden of collective shame
Term names shame as the intended inner-corrective response to moral failure — apostolic rebuke aimed at producing inner shame that prompts repentance
Term names shame as an inner state of disgrace and vulnerability — experienced in social reversal, chosen through suffering, perverted as grounds for boasting, or avoided through moral integrity
Term names the experience of shame as an inward condition of mortification — arising from sin, failure, or covenant breach before God or community
Term names shame as a social condition imposed upon an enemy or adversary — functioning as the opposite of honor and as the visible marker of defeat or rejection
Term names shame as the inner consequence of idolatry — the people put to shame through their misplaced devotion
Term names shame as a covering experience — shame that covers the face or envelops the person as the experience of disgrace and judgment
Term names perpetual, permanent shame — the enduring inner-social condition of disgrace that outlasts any possibility of restoration
Term names shame as an inward condition of dishonour and disgrace, covering the person before God and before the community
Term names shameful conduct and motivation — where the inner moral standard of shame governs what is done (shameful deeds) and what drives it (shameful gain)
Term names inner callousness — the loss of moral sensitivity that opens the inner person to sensuality and every kind of impurity
Term names the relational act of evoking shame or respect — shame as an inner corrective aimed at moral repentance, and respect as the inner deference owed to another's authority or dignity
Term names public disgrace as an expression of contempt — the act of exposing to open shame directed at Christ, expressing the inner moral state of apostasy
Term names the act of dishonouring as a violation of inner-relational worth — contempt directed at persons or God that damages relational standing and inner dignity
Term names being put to shame or vindicated as an inner relational state — hope does not shame the one who holds it; faith brings vindication; boastful conduct brings shame
Term names mistreatment that violates inner-relational dignity — the experience of shame through insolent treatment, endured by servants and apostles for the sake of their commission
Term names the act of shaming another — the imposition of social-inner disgrace as a consequence of exposed wrongdoing

Term names the act of taunting or reviling as an expression of contempt, defiance, or hostility directed at persons — the verbal act originates in an inner orientation of contempt and produces inner-being impact on the recipient
Term names the act of taunting or defying God — expressing an inner orientation of contempt and arrogance toward the divine, and functioning as the ground of divine judgment
Terror spread in the land of the living — the dread imposed by military powers upon subject populations as an inner-being condition of the dominated
Terror as inner experience — the dread felt by the person, whether before God or at the threat of enemies; including terror directed toward and away from the person in relation to divine presence
Sudden panic and terror as inner affliction — the state of dismay, panic, and anguish that falls upon persons, including the heart-aching dimension of divine visitation
Terror as the dashing of hope — the inner experience of terror that arrives where peace and healing were expected; the bitter reversal of inner expectation
Divine terror as an inner state fallen upon persons — the God-sent terror that falls on surrounding peoples, producing an inner condition that prevents hostile action
Terrors as overwhelming inner assault — plural terrors that attack, chase, overtake, and frighten the inner being, including the terror of deep darkness and the king of terrors
Sheer terror as the inner response to understanding divine judgement — the trembling dread that arises from comprehension of the divine message
Restricting or intensifying inner-being content — the particle qualifying inner states, moral orientation, or cognitive content by restriction or intensification
Term names cursing as an inner-being act — directed against parents, God, rulers, or God's anointed — with moral and covenantal weight
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names contempt as the inner disposition that evaluates another as light/worthless — looking down on persons, treating things as trivial, or despising God's commands
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the lightening of burden or minimising of difficulty — in its inner-being dimension: divine perspective on difficulty, false minimising of spiritual wounds, or the expansion of divine commission
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the contempt of surrounding nations toward Israel — and God's judgment that removes and avenges that contempt
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names contempt as the inner disposition that evaluates another as worthless — directed at fellow believers, the weak, or at Christ himself — and its reversal in God's economy
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names despising as an inner act with moral and covenantal consequence — directed at birthright, God's word, God's name, or God's servant — and the divine response of honor or shame
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names contempt as a social-inner contagion — one act of defiance spreading the inner disposition of contempt through a whole society
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names dishonour as the inner-social condition where contempt inverts proper relational honour — and divine judgment as its agent
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names abhorrence and everlasting contempt as the ultimate inner-being condition of those who rebel against God — the final state of the unrighteous
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the contempt directed at the Son of Man — the inner-being experience of rejection and disgrace borne as the path of the suffering servant
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of swerving toward the proud and the lie — the misdirection of trust that the blessed person avoids
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names folly as the inner moral disposition that commits outrageous acts against covenant, personhood, and God — the "disgraceful thing done in Israel"
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored

groups.context_description: Term names lewdness as the inner condition of spiritual adultery — exposed by God before the lovers it sought
groups.notes: Description updated to match verse_context_group correction — VCB-022 80-char truncation restored
